title: COMPETITION NOTICE FOR THE ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS ACADEMIC YEAR 2025/2026
summary: This document is a competition notice issued under the PNRR and financed by the European Union’s Next Generation EU programme for the academic year 2025/2026. Its main purpose is to outline the procedures and criteria for awarding scholarships and accommodation benefits to eligible university students. Key topics covered include eligibility requirements, types and duration of benefits, application methods, deadlines, and provisions for disabled students.

structure.title: Article 1 – Benefits of the Competition Notice
Article 2 – Eligible students
structure.text:
## Article 2 – Eligible students

1. Students who are eligible for the benefits under this notice, limited to the achievement of the first degree for each level of study, are those enrolled for a.y. 2025/2026 in: a) the following full-time courses run by the University of Genoa (hereafter “University”):

-  first level bachelor degree courses, single-cycle master's degree courses, second level master's degree courses;
-  specialisation courses, at least two years in duration, with an exception for the medical area according to Italian Legislative Decree no. 368 of 17 August 1999; PhD courses activated according to Italian Legislative Decree no. 210 of 3 July 1998, Art. 4who do not benefit from the university scholarship;


b) study courses run by the Institutions for Higher Education in Art and Music (AFAM), based in Liguria, pursuant to Table B attached to this notice:

 first-level specialist degree;  second-level specialist degree (or equivalent);  previous system degree.

- 2. The title obtained for the completion of studies at Fine Arts Academies, State Schools of Music and equivalent musical Institutes (Current System) and at the end of university diploma courses, is conventionally considered as equivalent to the bachelor degree (first level title) and, as such, enables the student to compete for the concession of a scholarship and accommodation only for a master's degree, or a single-cycle master's degree, as long as, in this latter case, admission has taken place or takes place during years after the first in accordance with Article 3.5 paragraph 1.
- 3. The previous system degree (hereinafter also referred to as the “PS degree”) is conventionally considered equivalent to a master's degree (second level title) and allows the student to compete for the scholarship and accommodation but only in order to achieve the specialisation or PhD courses.
- 4. The diploma obtained after completion of previous system studies, completed at State Schools of Music and equivalent Musical Institutes, entitles the student to compete for a scholarship for any three-year course, as long as it is not run by the Institutes in question.

structure.title: Article 3 - Duration of the concession of the benefits
structure.text: ## Article 3 - Duration of the concession of the benefits

nodes.title: 3.1 Scholarship
nodes.text:
### 3.1 Scholarship

1. Students may obtain a scholarship for the first title, only for each level of study, for a maximum time, calculated from the year of first enrolment, of:

a) if enrolled in first-level bachelor degree courses: a total of 7 semesters (6 semesters plus an additional semester); b) if enrolled in single-cycle master's degree courses: a total of 11 semesters for students enrolled in courses with a legal duration of 5 years, a total of 13 semesters for students enrolled in courses with a legal duration of 6 years (a period corresponding to the duration provided by the respective educational systems plus an additional semester); c) if enrolled in second-level master's degree courses: a total of 5 semesters (4 semesters plus an additional semester); d) if enrolled in AFAM Institutions: period corresponding to the legal duration of the courses; e) if enrolled in PhD courses or specialisation courses: duration established by the respective teaching systems.

nodes.title: 3.2. Accommodation
nodes.text:
### 3.2. Accommodation

1. The duration of the free concession of accommodation limited to the first degree for each level of study, for a maximum period of time which is calculated from the year of first enrolment, is equal to:

a) if enrolled in first-level bachelor degree courses: a total of 8 semesters (6 semesters, plus 2 semesters); b) if enrolled in single-cycle master’s degree courses: a total of 12 semesters for students enrolled in courses with a legal duration of 5 years, a total of 14 semesters for students enrolled in courses with a legal duration of 6 years (duration established by the respective teaching systems plus 2 semesters); c) if enrolled in second-level master's degree courses: a total of 6 semesters (4 semesters plus 2 semesters); d) if enrolled in the Institutions for High Artistic, Musical and Performing Arts Education: duration established by the legal duration of the courses; e) if enrolled in for PhD courses or specialisation courses: a period corresponding to the duration established by the respective didactic regulations.

- 2. For students referred to in letters a), b), c) of section 3.1 paragraph 1, the second additional semester is granted against payment of €200,00 per month if in ALiSEO Residences.
-

nodes.title: 3.3. Scholarship for disabled students
3.4 Accommodation for disabled students
nodes.text:
### 3.4 Accommodation for disabled students

1. The duration of the free concession of accommodation service, limited to the first degree for each level of study, for a maximum period of time, calculated from the year of first enrolment, is equal to:

a) if enrolled in first degree courses: a total of 10 semesters (6 semesters plus 4 additional semesters); b) if enrolled in single-cycle master’s degree courses: a total of 14 semesters for students enrolled in courses with a legal duration of 5 years, a total of 16 semesters for students enrolled in courses with a legal duration of 6 years (duration established by the respective teaching systems plus 4 semesters); c) if enrolled in second-level master's degree courses: a total of 8 semesters (4 semesters plus 4 semesters); d) if enrolled in the Institutions for Higher Education in Art and Music (AFAM):: duration established by the legal duration of the courses; e) if enrolled in for PhD courses or specialisation courses: duration established by the respective teaching systems.

- 2. For students referred to in letters a), b), c) of section 3.3 paragraph 1, the last additional semester is granted against payment of € 200,00 per month if in ALiSEO Residences.
-

nodes.title: 3.5 Clarifications on the duration of the benefits
nodes.text:
### 3.5 Clarifications on the duration of the benefits


- 1. For students who, after attending a bachelor's degree program or a conventionally corresponding (with or without obtaining a degree) are admitted or have been admitted to a single-cycle master's degree program, for the purpose of the duration of the granting of benefits, are counted all years of enrollment from the conventional year of first enrollment in the bachelor's degree, including 2025/2026, and any years of non-enrollment between courses (bachelor's and one-cycle master's degree).
- 2. These students may obtain a scholarship and accommodation for a maximum period equal to the difference between the normal duration of the single-cycle master's degree, increased by one semester (two semesters for accommodation – or two semesters for the scholarship and three semesters for the accommodation, in case of students with disabilities referred to in Article 13 of this Competition Notice and the number of years taken to achieve the study title in their possession.
- 3. Students who, having achieved a university diploma that allowed them to be admitted to a threeyear degree or a single cycle master's degree, may take part in the selection for a number of semesters equal to the difference between the normal duration of three-year or the single-cycle master's degree, increased by one semester (or three semesters in case of students with disabilities referred to in Article 13 of this Competition Notice and the number of semesters taken to achieve the university diploma.
- 4. Students who have obtained the equivalence of a degree achieved abroad cannot apply for the benefits referred to in this Notice for an equivalent level degree, without prejudice to the application of the provisions referred to in the previous paragraphs.

structure.title: Article 4 - Definitions of the type of students
structure.text:
## Article 4 - Definitions of the type of students

- 1. The term “on-site student” is used to refer to students residing in the municipality in which the attended course of studies is based.
- 2. The term “off-site student” is used to refer to students residing in a municipality that is distant from the place of the attended course and, therefore, request accommodation near the university, using the ALiSEO residential structures and, in case of unavailable free rooms, submit a regular housing contract following the instructions of the art. 22 of the Notice of Competition. Off-site students who request accommodation service must be at least eighteen years of age on the date of the application deadline.


- If students residing in municipalities that are considered as “off-site” with respect to the university locations (pursuant to

nodes.title: TABLE A
nodes.text:
#### TABLE A
attached to this notice) should not request accommodation in the residential structures of ALiSEO, they shall be considered as “Commuting” students.
- 3. The term “commuting student” is used to refer to students who are not considered as off-site and yet reside in a municipality other than that of the place of the course of studies attended (see Table A attached to this Notice), who do not require accommodation in the ALiSEO residential structures or in residential structures holding agreements with ALiSEO (in accordance with what stated before).
- 4. The online application for benefits provides an automatic system for determining the type of student in function of the location of the attended course of study and of the municipality of residence of the student. This information must be declared by the student in the application.
- 5. If the "off-site" students renounce the benefit of the accommodation place or are ineligible for the rental contribution (referred to in Article 22 of this Notice), they receive the disbursement of the monetary installment of the scholarship as a "commuter."
- 6.Students who believe that they should actually be classified differently, on the basis of the travel time necessary to reach the municipality where the attended course of studies is based using public means of transport, they may request their status to be reviewed at the same time as submitting their application for a scholarship. To this purpose, they must therefore submit a specific, duly documented request to this end, showing the time taken to reach the municipality where the attended course of studies is based, from their place of residence. The Head of the Department Right to Study of ALiSEO evaluates the request at his sole discretion within the date as indicated in the art. 23 paragraph 6 of the Notice.
- 7. The international non-EU students residing in Italy and applying for a place in ALiSEO's residential facilities are considered to be “off-site” with the exception of those whose household resides in Italy. In this case, in order to determine the “typology” of the student the actual residence of the family unit is considered,.
- 8. The “independent” student, referred to in art. 11.4.1 of this Competition Notice, is considered to be “off-site” regardless of their place of residence, in accordance with the provisions of Ministry of Universities and Research Decree no. 1320 of 17 December 2021.

structure.title: Article 5 - Conventional year of first registration, provisions for changes of course or renunciation of studies
Article 6 - Method for the submission of on-line applications for the assignment of benefits
structure.text:
## Article 6 - Method for the submission of on-line applications for the assignment of benefits

- 1. The application to take part in the competition for the assignment of ALiSEO benefits for academic year 2024/2025 must only be submitted by the online procedure on the website: www.aliseo.liguria.it – Student webdesk.

- 2. According to Decree-Law No. 76 of July 16, 2020, converted into Law No. 120 of 11 September 2020, as of 1 March 2021, in order to access services and benefits offered by the Public Administration, it is necessary to be in possession of SPID credentials (at least level 2) or the Electronic Identity Card (CIE).

nodes.title: 6.1. Exceptions to the use of SPID/CIE (until new regulatory dispositions):
nodes.text:
### 6.1. Exceptions to the use of SPID/CIE (until new regulatory dispositions):

- 1. Students who on the date of application are not in possession of the Italian citizenship and a valid Italian identity document are exempted from the requirement to have SPID credentials (at least level
- 2) or the Electronic Identity Card (CIE) and can therefore access the online "Accreditation" procedure (registration on the ALiSEO website). These students, during the accreditation process, must upload the following valid documents:


a) for EU citizens: identity card (front and back); b) for non-EU citizens: passport.

Under-age students with Italian citizenship can access the online procedure using the Electronic Identity Card (CIE).

2. At the end of the accreditation process, a personal code (user code) is sent to the email indicated by the student, which is used only when logging in for the first time. Through the use of this code the student can access the forms for requesting the benefits by performing the “Login” and then clicking on “Domanda Benefici Studenti”. For further access to the online procedure, the student must use his/her own tax code (Fiscal Code) as username and the password he/she entered in the “Accreditation” procedure.

nodes.title: 6.2 How to apply for benefits
nodes.text:
### 6.2 How to apply for benefits

1. To fill in the application form for benefits, the student must have and know: a) Student enrolment number/ID student number; b) Fiscal code; c) SPID or CIE identity or valid student ID identified in the paragraph “Exceptions to the use

of SPID/CIE (until new regulatory requirements)”; d) E-mail address (see comma 3); e) Income documentation (see Article 11); f) IBAN code of the current account or prepaid card, Italian or SEPA area, This account must be entitled to the student or has to be a joint account of the student requesting the benefit; g) Number of achieved credits/years registered by University/AFAM at the date of 10 August 2025 and the marks arithmetic average. This information is requested only to students enrolled in subsequent years (see Article 9);

h) Valid Italian certification attesting the recognition of disability pursuant to art. 3 paragraph 1 of Law n. 104 of 5 February 1992, or disability equal to or greater than 66%, for students

applying for "Integration of students with recognition of disability (art. 3 paragraph 1 Law n. 104/92) or disability equal to or greater than 66%" (art. 13 of this Announcement);

i) Identity card, driving licence or passport; j) a recent high quality passport photo in document size that enables to clearly identify the

student.

2. Once logged-in, students must: a) Fill in the online form, attaching the requested certifications; b) Verify the correctness of the submitted data; c) Submit the application; d) Verify that the application has been correctly sent by verifying the receipt of the e-mail

attesting the protocol number of the application; e) Print or save the application receipt.

3. During the online registration students are requested to insert an e-mail address. The student should be aware that:

a) the student is identified, regarding e-mail correspondence, through the e-mail address present in the ALiSEO database; b) the e-mail address in the ALiSEO database is attributed to the student and must be managed by the student and not by any other party; c) all communications between the student and ALiSEO and vice versa must be made to the address entered in the ALiSEO database; d) failure to read communications sent to the student by ALiSEO does not constitute acceptable reason for possible contestation; e) email sent by the student from addresses other than the one entered in the ALiSEO database will not be taken into consideration and therefore will not be answered by AliSEO; f) any change in the e-mail address must be made by the student through the appropriate procedure available on the student's personal page "Student Webdesk".

- 4. The application can be correctly submitted if the student has the receipt of the application with the protocol number related to the application. This receipt does not represent the eligibility for the benefits: the result of the application will be available with the publication of the provisional ranking lists and, after those, with the definitive ranking lists.
- 5. Students must verify the correctness of the attached files. Documents must be entirely scanned and must be readable in all their parts. Accepted formats are PDF, JPEG, PNG o GIF, for a maximum size of 5Mbytes each. If damaged files (not readable) have been attached, those files will not be considered valid for the competition.
- 6. The access codes to apply for benefits are strictly personal and the application must therefore be completed exclusively by the student who requests the benefits and takes full responsibility for the declared information.

nodes.title: 6.3 Modifying the information in the application form
nodes.text:
### 6.3 Modifying the information in the application form

1. Students can modify the information inserted in the online application form: a) within the deadline dates for the submission of the application:

- • the student who has not finished to fill in the application form and therefore has not yet submitted the online application can re-enter the online procedure. In order to finalize and submit the online application the student must follow the provisions in art. 11.3.1 of this notice.
- • the student who, after having submitted the online application, wants to modify the declared information or the attached files, can re-enter the application form and, once the


modifications are done, submit again the application following the provisions in art. 11.3.1 of this notice.

b) after the deadline dates for the submission of the application:

- • the student can modify information as far as personal data and IBAN code are concerned, reentering in the “Student webdesk/Sportello Studenti” page;
- • any other event which may happen after the submission of the application must be immediately notified to ALiSEO using the e-mail address borsestudio@aliseo.liguria.it. ALiSEO will check the requirements subsistence or will decide for a revocation order of the benefits. If not, ALiSEO reserves the right to order measures as far as the omission of communication is concerned.

ALiSEO authorizes the request upon receipt of a copy of the application for benefits from the University Study Institution of the University or AFAM Institution of origin.


3. Students can ask for information and assistance in filling in the on-line application by calling 840.84.80.38 within the following hours: from Monday to Friday, 9.00 to 12.00 noon, from 11 June 2025 to 31 July 2025. The cost of the call is € 0.10 excluding VAT, per connection fee.

structure.title: Article 8 - Students registered for the 1st year (1st and 2nd level) ‐ Requirements for merit
structure.text:
## Article 8 - Students registered for the 1st year (1st and 2nd level) ‐ Requirements for merit

- 1. Students who want to enrol for the first time in a first-year course (without prejudice of article 5), in order to apply for the benefits must be in possession of the economic requirements as per article 11 of this Notice. At the moment of the online submission the merit requirements are not requested. This requirement, if the student is awarded with a scholarship, must be attained by the student during the academic year as specified in the following paragraph.
- 2. Students awarded with a scholarship during the academic year 2025/2026 must achieve 20 credits obtained and registered by University or AFAM Institutions, by the dates below specified, in order to receive the payment of the scholarship. To these students the amount is paid as follows:


- a) first year students of the bachelor degree and first year students of one-cycle master's degree: 20% of the whole scholarship amount by 10 November 2025; 30% of the whole scholarship amount by 31 December 2025; 50% of the whole scholarship amount at the achievement of 20 credits by 10 August 2026.
- b) first – year of Master’s Degree students: 50% of the whole scholarship amount by 31 December 2025; 50% of the whole scholarship amount at the achievement of 20 credits by 10 August 2026.


- 3. The payment of the amounts mentioned above in paragraph a) referring to 20% and 30% and to the amount of the first instalment of 50% mentioned in paragraph b) will consist firstly, for “commuting” and “off-site” students, in the fee service of the scholarship (one free meal in the Canteen centres and free accommodation for the a.y 2025/2026).
- 4. Should the part paid in services not be sufficient to cover the value of 20% and/or 50% of the scholarship, ALiSEO will provide the remaining monetary difference.
- 5. For students enrolled in Health Professions degree courses, related to the School of Medical and Pharmaceutical Sciences, for the purpose of calculating the 20 credits, the credits from the technical internship examination scheduled for the 2025/2026 academic year are also taken into account if obtained and registered by the University in the student’s section “Examination self-certification” by 31 October 2026.


- 6. Scholarship winners who achieve the 20 credits by 30 November 2026 are entitled to maintain 50% of the total amount of the scholarship disbursed and are not entitled to the payment of the further 50%.
- 7. Scholarship winners who have not obtained at least 20 credits by 30/11/2026 will have their benefits withdrawn and will have to reimburse the costs of any services enjoyed (accommodation and catering).
- 8. The Manager of the ALiSEO Right to Study Sector, in exceptional cases, upon justified, duly documented request submitted to the Economic Benefits Service from 1/12/2026 to 31/12/2026 may defer, at his sole discretion, by no more than three months the deadline for achieving the minimum merit levels required to avoid revocation.
- 9. Students with disabilities referred to in Article 13 of this Competition Notice who have been awarded a scholarship are not required to achieve the merit requirement in order to receive the monetary portion of the scholarship. Therefore, to such students, the scholarship shall be disbursed in a lump sum by December 31st, 2025.
- 10. It is specified that, for all degree courses, credits are considered as achieved and therefore valid for the purposes of the merit requirement if they are included in the student's career and registered in the examination certificate with a date equal to or before 10/8/2026 or 30/11/2026 issued by the University or AFAM Institutions, with an indication of their acquisition no later than the aforementioned date. The following cannot be calculated in the sum of credits


a) credits deriving from training debts; b) credits from modules in progress that require further integration for the final grade; c) credits from individual courses that are not recognised; d) supernumerary credits, i.e. those not useful for obtaining the degree; e) credits deriving from a bachelor degree obtained abroad and validated by the University for

a master degree course.

8.1 Students enrolled in the first years of study courses in Medicine and Surgery and Dentistry and Dental Prosthodontics at the University of Genoa

- 1. Students enrolling for the ‘filter’ semester in the single-cycle degree courses in Medicine and Surgery LM-41 and Dentistry and Dental Prosthodontics LM-46 at the University of Genoa must apply for benefits to Aliseo, in accordance with the procedures and requirements set out in this competition notice, by the deadline of 12 noon on 31/7/2025.
- 2. The students referred to in paragraph 1 and who are matriculated at the University of Genoa:


- a) to the second semester of the single-cycle degree courses in Medicine and Surgery LM-41 and Dentistry and Dental Prosthetics LM-46 (as they are classified in a useful position in the national ranking);
- b) second semester of the related course referred to in Article 8, paragraph 1 of Ministerial Decree no. 418 of 30 May 2025, indicated at the time of enrolment for the filter semester (as they were not placed in a useful position in the national ranking);


are included in a special ranking list that will be published in accordance with the timeframe in which the national ranking list drawn up by the MUR is issued. As regards the procedures for the use of accommodation and catering services in the ‘filter’ semester and for the procedures for the possible awarding of the scholarship and the use of accommodation and catering services in the second semester, please refer to subsequent indications following the issuing of the ministerial decree referred to in art. 4 paragraph 3 of legislative decree no. 71 of 15 May 2025.

3. Students who have been enrolled in the ‘filter’ semester at other Italian Universities and are enrolled at the University of Genoa in the

- a) the second semester of the single-cycle degree courses in Medicine and Surgery LM41 and Dentistry and Dental Prosthodontics LM-46 (as they were ranked in the national rankings)
- b) second semester of the related course referred to in Article 8, paragraph 1 of Ministerial Decree no. 418 of 30 May 2025, indicated at the time of enrolment in the “filter” semester (as they are not ranked in a useful position in the national ranking;


they must submit an application for benefits within the deadline that will be determined in accordance with the timeframe in which the national ranking list drawn up by the MUR is issued, by contacting the Economic Benefits Service at the following email address: borsestudio@aliseo.liguria.it.

The application must be submitted in accordance with the procedures set out in this Notice and students must be in possession of an ISEEU/ISEEUP 2025 certification whose indicators must not exceed the limits set out in art. 11.2. The students referred to in this paragraph are included in the same ranking referred to in paragraph 2 above. For the possible allocation of the study grant and the use of accommodation and catering services in the second semester, please refer to subsequent indications subsequent to the issuing of the ministerial decree referred to in art. 4 paragraph 3 of legislative decree no. 71 of 15 May 2025.

a) Credits deriving from educational debts; b) Credits of in itinere activities which require supplements for the final note; c) Credits of single courses if not validated; d) Any excess credits, i.e. the ones not required for the achievement of the title; e) Credits deriving from a first-level degree obtained abroad and validated by the University

- 3. Students enrolled in degree courses in medical professions, run by the School of Medical and Pharmaceutical Sciences, may specify in advance (during the application for the benefits) the credits achieved by practical traineeships that will be considered under reservation pending registration by the University of Genoa by 31 October 2025. If the registration is made after this date, said credits will not be considered valid and therefore the student’s position will be reconsidered for the publication of the final ranking list.
- 4. Students who have achieved credits during their international mobility period can indicate in advance in the application the relevant credits which will be considered under reservation pending registration by University or AFAM Institutions. Exams achieved during international mobility must be registered in the exam certification, released by University and AFAM Institutions, with the indication of their achievement within the date of 10 August 2025. Should the registration be made after the said date, the credits will not be considered valid and therefore the student’s position will be reconsidered.
- 5. Any variation following the deadline for the submission of the application for the benefits related to the merit requirement must be promptly communicated to ALiSEO's Economic Benefits Service, at the following email address borsestudio@aliseo.liguria.it, which will verify the preservation of the requirement.

- 6. Any credits achieved and registered between August 1st and August 10th 2025 that have not been declared in the online application have to be notified to ALiSEO’s Economic Benefits Service at the following email address borsestudio@aliseo.liguria.it, by 29 August 2025.

nodes.title: 9.1.1 Bonus credits
nodes.text:
### 9.1.1 Bonus credits

- 1. Students who fail to achieve the minimum number of credits referred to Tables 3 and 4 within the date of 10 August 2025 may use a bonus in addition to the credits effectively achieved.
- 2. Bonus credits are achieved from the conventional year of first enrolment to all University courses (first-level bachelor degree, single-cycle master’s degree and second level master’s degree), as follows:


• 5 credits bonus for the application of the benefits for a second year student; • 12 credits bonus for the application of the benefits for a third year student ; • 15 credits bonus for the application of the benefits for a subsequent third year student .

3. Specifications about the bonus credits: a) The bonus value increases only if the bonus is not used, up to a maximum of 15 bonus credits; b) Individual bonuses are not cumulative;

c) A bonus fully used by students results in the loss of the right to use other bonus points for

the rest of their academic career; d) The residual bonus amount can be used in the following years; e) The bonus acquired and not used in the bachelor degree courses may be used if the student

registers for a second level specialised/master's degree courses (max. 15 credits). The student will not achieve new bonus points during the second level master’s degree;

f) The bonus used to achieve the minimum of credits for the academic year of application for benefits cannot be counted by the student within the range of the credits achieved to obtain merit in the following year.

- 4. Students must apply for the bonus during the online application in the section “Merit Conditions”. The bonus, if still available, will be automatically assigned. The Economic Benefits Service reserves the right to make further verification for the availability of the bonus credits.
- 5. The student coming from other Italian Universities or from other AFAM institutions applying for the bonus credits must indicate in the online application the name of the University of origin. The Economic Benefits Service reserves the right to verify automatically the bonus self-certified by the student.
- 6. Bonus credits can not be used by the following students: a) enrolled in previous system courses; b) enrolled in the first year of first and second level courses to achieve the merit requirement as


per article 8 paragraph 2 (20 CFU); c) enrolled in a first year of a master’s degree course to achieve 135 credits that allow the right to the accommodation service as a confirmation student.

nodes.title: 9.3 Seventh semester and first year of a master's degree ‐ admission and merit requirements
nodes.text:
### 9.3 Seventh semester and first year of a master's degree ‐ admission and merit requirements

1. The application for the “seventh semester and first year of a master's degree” allows students meeting the requirements of 135 CFU achieved and registered by University within 10 August 2025 (or 114 CFU for disabled students) to submit an application for benefits for two different levels of courses of study at a single time. Only students in the following position may submit this type of application:

a. students registered in academic year 2022/2023 (2021/2022 for disabled students) in a

bachelor degree at University; b. students not graduated by 31 July 2025; c. students who register for the academic year 2025/2026 for a first-year master's Degree or for

a fourth year bachelor degree.

Students who do not jointly satisfy the above a), b) and c) conditions, must verify their requirements for the application for the “last semester” (see paragraph 9.4) or for “first year master’s degree” (article 8 of this Notice). The student who has already obtained a bachelor degree at the moment of submitting the application, or who will obtain it by 31 July 2025, must apply for the benefits as a “first year master’s degree student”.

- 2. The application for benefits for the “seventh semester and first year of a master's degree” must be submitted using the specific option available in the on-line application (section “Cosa studi”). At first, students shall consecutively fill in the fields relevant to the seventh semester of the degree course and thereafter those for the first year of the master's degree.
- 3. Merit requirements to obtain/preserve benefits for the “seventh semester and first year of a master's degree” are the following:


 for the seventh semester while submitting the application, the requirements stated in Tables 3 and 4 of the previous paragraph 9.1 of this Notice;  for the first year of the master’s degree, they must be achieved during the academic year in accordance with what stated in the previous article 8 of this Notice.

4. Students awarded with the scholarship will be paid a first instalment which equals to 25% of the net amount of the scholarship (see Table 8 article 17 in this Notice), possible supplements included, by 31 December 2025. The second instalment will be paid according to the following provisions:

a) Students enrolled for a first year of a master’s degree for the academic year 2025/2026:

- • on the achievement of 20 credits within 10 August 2026: 75% of the amount of which at Table 8 article 17 of this Notice, possible supplements included;
- • on the achievement of 20 credits within 30 November 2026: 25% of the amount of which at Table 8 article 17 of this Notice, possible supplements included. b) Students enrolled to a seventh semester (fourth year of a first level degree) for the academic year 2025/2026


◦ 25% of the net amount of which at Table 8-9 of article 17 of this Notice, possible supplements included within 30 June 2026.

Students referred to in paragraph 5 lettera a) who have been awarded scholarships and who do not achieve at least 20 credits by 30 November 2026 will have their benefits revoked and will have to reimburse the amount of the scholarship unduly received and the costs of the services they have benefited (accommodation and catering).

5. Students who have been awarded a scholarship for “seventh semester and first year of a master's degree”, who:

- a. may not enrol to the first year of a master’s degree for the academic year 2025/2026 by 10 March 2026, will be considered awarded of a “last semester” scholarship;
- b. are enrolled to the first year of a master’s degree for the academic year 2025/2026, may only compete as students registered for the second year of a second level master's degree in the academic year 2026/2027.

nodes.title: 9.4 Last semester – Access and merit requirements
nodes.text:
### 9.4 Last semester – Access and merit requirements

- 1. The application for the “last semester” is reserved to students possessing the requirements referred to at Tables 3 and 4 article 9 (requested merit for “last semester”) and who are enrolled for the academic year 2025/2026 in the first year beyond the legal duration of the course: Bachelor degree, single-cycle master degree and second level master’s degree.
- 2. The students awarded with a “last semester” scholarship are entitled to:  50% of the amounts as per table 8, article 17 of the present Notice, included possible supplements;  a free meal per day at the canteen service, starting from the final ranking and until 31st


March 2026;  a free accommodation, if assignee, and until 31st March 2025.  free accommodation or a rental contribution if eligible, from the date of publication of the

rankings of the rankings until 31/3/2026.

It is agreed that from April 1st 2026, the student awarded a semester scholarship is required to pay for the meals at the price applicable to his “bracket” (see Catering Service Regulation) and for the monthly rent of 200,00 € until completion of the degree. 3.Students who apply as ‘last semester’ for the 2025/2026 academic year and graduate in the 2024/2025 academic year (current graduates) will lose their benefits.

structure.title: Article 10 - Requirements of merit for students registered for doctorate and specialisation courses
structure.text:
## Article 10 - Requirements of merit for students registered for doctorate and specialisation courses

1. As for doctorates pursuant the Legislative Decree n.210 of 03 July 1998, art. 4 and specialisation courses, apart from those of the medical area, pursuant to Italian Legislative Decree no. 368 of 17 August 1999 (first year and following years), students must be validly enrolled and admitted to

courses in the ways envisaged by the respective teaching systems without any surpassing of the prescribed course duration or repeating from the year of first registration.

structure.title: Article 11 – Economical requirements
structure.text: ## Article 11 – Economical requirements

nodes.title: 11.1 University ISEE/Parified ISEE
nodes.text:
### 11.1 University ISEE/Parified ISEE

- 1. The student’s economic conditions are identified by Prime Ministerial Decree 159 of 05/12/2013 and subsequent amendments and additions on ISEE and completed by the criteria envisaged by Prime Ministerial Decree 9 April 2001, by article 5 and by Articles 7 and 8 of the Legislative Decree n. 68 of 29 March 2012.
- 2. In order to apply for the benefits, the student must mandatorily be in possession of the ISEE certificate for Subsidised Benefits for the Right to University Study (ISEEU/ISEEUP) as specified below.


-

nodes.title: 11.1.1 ISEEU - ISEE University Attestation
nodes.text:
### 11.1.1 ISEEU - ISEE University Attestation

- 1. It can be issued by all Authorised Tax Assistance Centres (CAAF) or it can be filled in on the INPS website.
- 2. For the purposes of participation in the competition for the awarding of benefits for the academic year 2025/2026, it is considered valid the most recent ISEEU 2025 certificate present in the INPS database, issued in favour of the student requesting the benefits pursuant to this Announcement, whose Dichiarazione Sostitutiva Unica (DSU) was signed by 31 July 2025.
- 3. In addition to the previous it is also considered valid the current ISEE statement, issued on the basis of the ISEEU 2025 statement with the aforementioned modalities and within the aforementioned deadline.
- 4. In the online application, students are required to allow ALiSEO to retrieve from the INPS database the parameters related to the last ISEEU 2025 certificate issued in their favour and requested not later than 31 July 2025.
- 5. In order to take part in the competition, students who by the application deadline are not yet in possession of the ISEE University certificate must at least posses the receipt certifying the submission of the “Dichiarazione Sostitutiva Unica” (DSU) to the CAAF.
- 6. If there is no DSU 2025 in the INPS database requested by 31 July 2025, the student will be DEFINITIVELY excluded from the competition in the provisional rankings.
- 7. Ordinary ISEE certificates (“ISEE ordinario”) and ISEEU certificates with annotations and/or discrepancies are not considered valid for the competition.
- 8. ISEEU certificates issued by 31 July 2025 containing annotations or discrepancies or “ordinary ISEE” certificates may be corrected and re-issued, under penalty of being DEFINITIVELY excluded from the competition, by 24 October 2025.


-

nodes.title: 11.1.2 ISEEUP - ISEE Parified University Certificate
nodes.text:
### 11.1.2 ISEEUP - ISEE Parified University Certificate


1. It can only be issued by Authorised Tax Assistance Centres (CAAF) that are affiliated with ALiSEO, the list of which is available on ALiSEO's institutional website.

- 2. In order to obtain the ISEEUP, the student must posses the consular documentation required by the CAAFs that have an agreement with AliSEO.
- 3. For the purposes of participation in the competition for the awarding of the benefits for the academic year 2025/2026, ISEEUP certificates issued by the affiliated CAAFs during the solar year 2025 by 24 October 2025 are considered valid.
- 4. Any student who submits an application for benefits within the deadline set out in the present Competition Notice, without being in possession of the ISEEUP 2025 certificate by 25 October 2025 will be included in the rankings in the status of "suspended" and may correct the situation not later than 31 December 2025, under penalty of being DEFINITIVELY revoked from the assigned benefits. In the on-line application, the student must compulsorily upload the ISEEUP 2025 certificate within the aforementioned deadlines.

nodes.text:

nodes.title: 11.2 Economical and income limitations – ISEEU – ISEEUP and ISPEU - ISPEUP
nodes.text:
### 11.2 Economical and income limitations – ISEEU – ISEEUP and ISPEU - ISPEUP


- 1. The ISEEU - ISEEUP of the student's family unit who is requesting the benefits provided by the present Application Requirements and Regulations must not exceed € 27.948,00 and the Equivalent Financial Situation Indicator ISPEU - ISPEUP (Indicatore della Situazione Patrimoniale Equivalente Universitaria) must not exceed € 60.757,00. The exceeding of even only one of the two aforementioned indicators is cause for exclusion from the benefits of the present Notice of Competition. The values of the economic indicators mentioned above and shown below in Table No. 6 are determined in compliance with the maximum values established by Decree No. 318 of the Ministry of Universities and Research of 28 February 2025.
- 2. If the student, while filling out the online application, in the appropriate box in the “economic data” section has indicated a scholarship amount provided by ALiSEO in the 2023 tax year, the

- 3. For the determination of the student's conventional family unit, to be declared in the DSU, reference is made to the regulations in force regarding ISEEU.
- 4. ALiSEO is in no way responsible for the failure of INPS/CAAF to forward the data contained in the DSU and/or the processing of the ISEEU/ ISEEUP for the application for benefits.


11.3 Income situations - international students

nodes.title: 11.3.1 Non-EU international students without a visa- access to the online application
nodes.text:
### 11.3.1 Non-EU international students without a visa- access to the online application

1. The non-EU international student who wishes to apply for ALiSEO benefits for the first year of the degree course, but is unable to obtain a visa to arrive in Italy by the deadline for submitting the application for benefits due to delays in the issuance of the VISA by the competent authorities, may still apply for benefits from abroad within the time limits set out in Article 7 of this Notice. Once the pre-enrolment procedure has been completed, the student, through the University's Foreigners Sector, receives the student ID number necessary to submit the online application for benefits from abroad. In the application for the benefits, the students are required to upload their valid passport. Students must:

- a) be in possession of ISEEUP certificate issued by an Authorised Tax Assistance Centre (CAAF) affiliated with ALISEO by the dates indicated in paragraph 11.1.2
- b) submit in the "Student Webdesk" section their IBAN code of an Italian bank or prepaid card, Italian or SEPA area. This account must be entitled to the student or has to be a joint account of the student requesting the benefit;
- c) upload the tax code attribution certificate issued by the Internal Revenue Service (Agenzia delle Entrate).

nodes.title: 11.3.2 Students from particularly poor and developing countries
nodes.text:
### 11.3.2 Students from particularly poor and developing countries

1. Students from particularly poor and developing countries as per decree of the Ministry of University and Research 3 March 2025, nr. 166, (see Table no. 7 below) must submit a certificate from the Italian Representation in their country of origin certifying that the student does not belong to a family known to be of high income and high social level. This certification, for the purpose of determining the amount of the University ISEEU and ISPEU is equivalent to € 0.00 for both indicators. The student when applying for benefits must declare that he/she is from "Particularly Poor and Developing Country" and also upload the above certification at the same time. In the absence of one or both of the above requirements, the student is not considered to be from "Particularly Poor and Developing Country."

Table n. 7 PARTICULARLY POOR AND DEVELOPING COUNTRIES

Afghanistan Angola Bangladesh Benin Burkina Faso Burundi Cambodgia Central African Republic Chad Comoros Democratic People’s Republic of Korea Democratic Republic of Congo Djibouti Eritrea Ethiopia Gambia Guinea Guinea - Bissau Haiti Kiribati Lao People’s Democratic Republic Lesotho Liberia Madagascar Malawi Mali

Mauritania Mozambique Myanmar Nepal Niger Rwanda Sao Tome and Principe Senegal Sierra Leone Solomon Islands Somalia South Sudan Sudan Syrian Arab Republic Tanzania Timor-Leste Togo Tuvalu Uganda Yemen Zambia

nodes.title: 11.3.3 Stateless and internationally protected students
nodes.text:
### 11.3.3 Stateless and internationally protected students

- 1. The stateless students when applying for benefits must declare that he/she is "Stateless" and at the same time upload a copy of the residence permit attesting their “stateless status” or, if in the absence of such documentation, must upload a certified copy of the sentence declaring their stateless status. In the absence of one or both of the above requirements, the student is not considered "Stateless."
- 2. Students under international protection when applying for benefits must declare that he/she is “ under international protection” and at the same time must upload a copy of their residence permit stating “political asylum”, “special protection”; if they are not yet in possession of the residence permit, students can submit the certificate of residence issued by the Police Headquarters or the decision issued by the competent Territorial Commission which recognizes the international protection status. In the absence of one or both of the above requirements, the student is not considered “under international protection”.
- 3. For the purposes of economic assessment for stateless students and students under international protection, only income and wealth produced in Italy will be taken into account. If students and their family unit, if any, do not have income in Italy, the calculation of the amount of the ISEEU and ISPEU indicators is made equal to € 0.00 for both indicators.


4. If other statuses in addition to those mentioned above are provided, they will be considered for income assessment purposes.

nodes.title: 11.4 Independent family unit
nodes.text: ### 11.4 Independent family unit

nodes.title: 11.4.1 Independent student
nodes.text:
### 11.4.1 Independent student

- 1. In order to apply for the benefits of this Notice, the student constitutes an independent family unit from that of the original family in the presence of both of the following requirements: a) personal residence outside the original family home for at least two years from the date of submission of the declaration of single replacement (DSU), in housing not owned by one of its members; b) Presence of an adequate capacity for taxed and assimilated income received in tax years 2023 and 2024, not be less than € 9,000.00 for each year. When applying for benefits, the student in possession of both the requirements referred to in a) and b) must declare that he/she is in the status of an "independent student."
- 2. In all the cases in which the students, even if no longer living in the original family, do not meet the above requirements to be considered as an independent student, they must compulsorily refer to the members of the original family unit and their economic and financial conditions.
- 3. For the issuance of the independent student's ISEEU/ISEEUP certificate, as by way of explanation and not exhaustive, the same indications regarding the family unit referred to in Table No. 5 with reference to the student himself/herself are valid.
- 4. The “independent” student is considered “off-site” regardless of the place of residence, in accordance with the provisions of Decree No. 1320 of the Ministry of University and Research December 17, 2021.

nodes.title: 11.4.2 Students who are married or in a de facto cohabitation regime (article 1 paragraph 36 law no. 76/2016) registered with the municipality of residence
nodes.text:
### 11.4.2 Students who are married or in a de facto cohabitation regime (article 1 paragraph 36 law no. 76/2016) registered with the municipality of residence

1. The married student, in a de facto cohabitation registered with the municipality of residence of the student, if he/she does not fit the condition to be considered as an “independent student” must add his/her own income to the one of his/her spouse (for each tax year 2023 and 2024).

- a) If he/she reaches the minimum amount of € 9.000,00 (for each tax year 2023 and 2024), the ISEEU/ISPEEU will therefore refer only to the nucleus that the student has established with his/her spouse, excluding the original family.
- b) If the income achieved by the married student and his/her spouse does not reach the minimum required limit of € 9.000,00 (for each tax year 2023 and 2024), the student must refer to the original family thus excluding the spouse and any children.

nodes.title: 11.4.3 Separated or divorced student
nodes.text:
### 11.4.3 Separated or divorced student

1. The separated or divorced students even if with children, if they do not meet the conditions to be considered "independent" must necessarily refer to the original family.

nodes.title: 11.4.4 Cases in which the family unit consists of only one student
nodes.text:
### 11.4.4 Cases in which the family unit consists of only one student

1. Students who do not meet the requirements to be considered “independent” can still submit the ISEEU/ISEEUP, related to their own economic condition, only if both their parents died or if they are living in a registered cohabitation in accordance with the art. 5 of the Presidential Decree 223/89. The categories provided for registered cohabitation are:

a) belonging to a religious order; b) belonging to a host community; c) subjected to a detention regime;

d) military; e) included in the protection programmes of anti-violence Centres.

- 2. A student belonging to a host community or a student included in the "care leavers" project can apply for the accommodation place at ALiSEO residences although he/she does not result in the condition of student "off site" in terms of his or her residence and course of study attended.
- 3. Students who are in a condition of “cohabitation” and married, for the elaboration of the ISEEU/ ISEEUP they must refer to the family unit established with their spouse and any children.
- 4. ALiSEO reserves the right to ask the student for appropriate documentation that certifies the conditions referred to in this paragraph 11.4.4.

nodes.title: 11.5 Economic requirements for students enrolled in PhD courses and schools of specialisation
nodes.text:
### 11.5 Economic requirements for students enrolled in PhD courses and schools of specialisation

1. Students requesting benefits for PhD courses and specialisation schools referred to in article 2 of this Notice, must comply with the provisions of the article 11 of this Notice. For these students, the family unit is formed only by the applicant student, the potential spouse, children also of legal age. It should be noticed that the small unit ISEE can also be requested by students whose family unit is made of their own original family. In this case it is necessary to submit an explicit request to the CAAF (Authorised Tax Assistance Centre) when completing the DSU by completing section A “restricted family unit for PhDs”.

structure.title: Article 12 – Supplements to the scholarship

Article 13 - Scholarship Integration and Benefits Facilitation for Students with Disabilities (recognition of disability pursuant to Article 3 paragraph 1 of Law No. 104 of February 5, 1992, or with disability of 66% or more)
Article 14 - International mobility contributions for students taking part in mobility programmes
structure.text:
## Article 14 - International mobility contributions for students taking part in mobility programmes

- 1. Only students who are winners in the relevant rankings drawn up by the University or AFAM Institutions may apply for the international mobility scholarship supplement. These students when applying for benefits, must tick the box “Supplement for international mobility self-certifying the country of destination and how many months they will spend abroad, until a maximum of ten months. Students enrolled in the first years of a second level specialised/master's degree, if they wish to apply for this supplement must declare to have achieved at least (during the bachelor degree) 135 credits by 10 August 2025.
- 2. The supplement as referred to in the previous section: a. cannot be granted to students enrolled in the first years of the bachelor’s degree and students


enrolled in specialization school courses and Ph.D. programs; b. can be granted only once for each level of study; c. can be granted for a maximum number of ten months spent abroad.

3.The amount of the scholarship supplement related to international mobility programs awarded by ALiSEO shall be € 600.00 monthly. From the above sum it is deducted the amount of the scholarship from EU funds (Community contribution) or other bilateral agreements, including non-EU agreements. International mobility contributions mentioned in this article are additional to the University “award for results” and is incompatible with the University or AFAM Institutions integration. ALiSEO also provides a total reimbursement of travel expenses determined as follows:

- - € 100.00 for European countries;
- - € 500.00 for non-European countries.


- 4. Students entitled to the integration of the scholarship related to international mobility programs will be identified on the basis of a ranking defined according to the criteria for determining the accommodation rankings and within the limits of the allocation of the budget.
- 5. In order to obtain the monetary amount referred to in paragraph 3 of this article, the student must obtain, during the period of study and/or internship abroad, academic recognition in terms of credits


within the framework of his or her course of study in Italy, even if for the purpose of the preparation of the final test.

6. The following provisions shall apply to the disbursement of the amount related to the international mobility scholarship supplement:

- a) the first instalment, corresponding to half of the monetary amount calculated on the basis of the number of months self-certified by the student when submitting the application and validated by the competent office of the University or AFAM Institutions, is paid, subject to verification that the student has signed the international mobility agreement, at the same time as the first instalment of the scholarship;
- b) the final instalment will only be paid upon completion of the study period and after the transmission by the University of Genoa and AFAM, to the Economic Benefits Service, of the certificate issued by the foreign university, declaring:


 the period spent abroad;  academic recognition in terms of credits within their course of study in Italy;  the amount paid to the student from European Union funds (EU contribution) or from

other bilateral agreements, including non-EU agreements.

7. ALiSEO calculates the amount of the final instalment on a monthly basis (30 days) in relation to the period actually spent abroad and deduced from the relevant certification.

If the aforementioned calculation, carried out on the actual period spent abroad, results in an amount lower than the amount already received by the student by way of the first instalment, the student is required to repay the difference for which he/she has not earned the right.

- 8. The amount of integration to the scholarship referred to in this article, for students placed in the "last semester" ranking can be disbursed up to a maximum of five months.
- 9. The student in international and national mobility (in Italy) must compulsorily and promptly notify the Canteen Service, (which will duly suspend the validity of the card for Canteen Service, in accordance with the Canteen Service Regulation) and the Accommodation Service (if student accommodated for the suspension of the accommodation according to the terms set out in Article 20 paragraph 5 of this Notice) using the specific form available from ALiSEO website in the section “Right to study”.
- 10. Students on international and national mobility (in Italy) in the 2025/2026 academic year are not entitled to any reimbursement for not using the services (Canteen Service and accommodation) as the amount of the “Mobility” supplement covers the cost of the above-mentioned services.
- 11. Students who are not winners in the rankings drawn up by the University or AFAM Institutions related to international mobility programs are excluded from the ALiSEO International Mobility integration ranking.
- 12. ALiSEO revokes the scholarship supplement for international mobility to students who do not meet the requirements set out in this article and to students for whom the University and AFAM institutions have notified the relevant service of their non-completion of the program or renunciation of the program.

structure.title: Article 15 - Criteria for the assignment of scholarships
structure.text:
## Article 15 - Criteria for the assignment of scholarships

1. Scholarships, insofar as available on the budget, are assigned by way of priority to students with disabilities referred to in art. 13 of this competition notice and, for 40 scholarships, to the students enrolled in specialisation schools, except for medical school in accordance with Legislative Decree no. 368 of 17 August 1999 and in PhD courses in accordance with the article 4 of the Legislative Decree no. 210 of 3 July 1998.

- 2. Resources available after this assignment are used to award scholarships to those registered for years after the first, for as long as there is budget availability.
- 3. If additional resources should remain available, within the limits of the budget, scholarships will be assigned to students registered for the first year of master's degrees.
- 4. If additional funds should remain available, scholarships will be assigned to students registered for the first year of bachelor degree courses and single-cycle masters degree in the form of services (Accommodation and Canteen Service).
- 5. Any resources as may remain after said assignment is used to award monetary portions of scholarships to students registered for the first year of first year of bachelor degree courses and single-cycle masters degree, according to the order listed in the rankings.

structure.title: Article 16 - Criteria for the formulation of scholarship ranking lists
Article 17 – Amounts of the scholarship and related services
structure.text: ## Article 17 – Amounts of the scholarship and related services

nodes.title: 17.1 Amounts
nodes.text:
### 17.1 Amounts

- 1. The gross amount of the scholarship varies according to the ISEEU/ISEEUP and to the type of student: “On-site”, “Commuting”, “Off-site” (article 4 of this Notice). The scholarship consists of the payment of a monetary portion in cash net of the annual value attributed to the canteen service (€ 1.148,00) and to the accommodation service (€ 2.527,00). The values of the scholarship are determined respecting the minimum values established by the Decree of the Ministry of University and Research 28 February 2025, nr. 181.
- 2. The Table no. 8 below shows the amounts relevant to the scholarship and the related services:


Table n. 8 SCHOLARSHIP AMOUNTS

Bracket assigned (according to University ISEEU/ISEEUP)

nodes.title: 17.2 Payment of the scholarship and provision of the services
17.3 Clarifications on scholarships and services
nodes.text:
### 17.3 Clarifications on scholarships and services

- 1. Students awarded scholarships who graduate in the academic year 2025/2026, maintain the right to the allocation of the monetary share of the scholarship (annual or semester) while they forfeit the services (accommodation and Canteen Service), as regulated by the relevant regulations/provisions. These students must therefore communicate to ALiSEO the achievement of the qualification within 10 days of the graduation session.
- 2. “Off-site” students awarded with the scholarship, but not accommodated due to unavailability of places, receive the scholarship increased by the amount corresponding to the value attributed to the housing service compared to the period of unavailability of the place with respect to the entire concession period, according to the criteria provided for in Article 22 of this Notice.
- 3. Students who are eligible in the provisional ranking lists of scholarships in the academic year 2025/2026 enrolled in the first years of all degree courses and subsequent years if they were not awarded scholarships for the academic year 2024/2025 are entitled to daily meals at the "Fascia 1" rate (see Provisions for Canteen Service) at the ALiSEO Canteen Service Centres, in the opening


- periods from the day following the publication of the provisional rankings until the publication of the final rankings.
- 4. Students whose status is “Suspended” in the provisional ranking lists of scholarships in the academic year 2025/2026 benefit from Canteen Service at the price “out of bracket” (see Provisions for Canteen Service) and cannot be accommodated. An exception is made for students who have applied for benefits for the first year of the master's degree course and who are “Suspended” due to non-enrolment in the 2025/2026 academic year, since they use the Canteen Service at the "Fascia 1" rate and can be accommodated if eligible for accommodation.
- 5. Students in the final rankings of scholarships in the academic year 2025/2026 whose status is “Suspended” benefit from the Canteen Service according to the “bracket” prices (see Provisions for Canteen Service) with the exception of students who have applied for benefits as a first year of a Master's Degree - 2nd level who are “Suspended” due to non-enrolment for the academic year 2025/2026 and benefit from the catering service at the price of “student awarded scholarships” (see Provisions for Canteen Service) and can be accommodated if eligible for accommodation.
- 6. Students who are eligible but not awarded the scholarship in the final rankings due to lack of funds are allowed to benefit for free from the services connected to the scholarship, as follows:


- a) Canteen Service: daily benefit from meals at the tariff applicable to "Fascia 1" rate at the ALiSEO Canteen Service Centres, in the opening periods from the day following the publication of the final rankings for the academic year 2025/2026 until the publication of the final rankings for the academic year 2026/2027;
- b) accommodation service (only if available): the benefit is granted according to the provisions of article 18 of this Notice.


- 7. Students who have resulted as winners, with reference to the type they belong to, in the final rankings of the 2025/2026 academic year scholarships attending courses at the University or AFAM Institutions at locations where there is no availability of:

- a) ALiSEO catering centres (exclusively for “commuting” and “off-site” students), the increase in the scholarship amount (catering contribution for decentralised locations) relative to the amount corresponding to the value attributed to the catering service (Table no. 8) for the period of unavailability of the catering service is automatically assigned;
- b) ALiSEO accommodation structures (exclusively for “off-site” students), may receive the scholarship increased by the amount of the contribution, according to the criteria provided for in Article 22 of this Notice;


- 8. There is no provision for any form of cashing in of services, nor is there any reimbursement for not eating meals during the opening and/or closing periods of the Canteen Service and/or for the total or partial failure to benefit from the accommodation service even during the closing periods of university residences, except for the situations listed below:


- a. for students who obtain the right to the payment of the rental contribution referred to in Article 22 of this Notice;
- b. for students with disability as referred to in Article 13 of this Notice whose non-use of the accommodation service depends on the provisions of Article 13 paragraph 2, letter g) of this Notice;
- c. for students attending decentralized locations of courses of study where free canteen service cannot be provided.
