structure.title: Article 19 - Criteria for the conferral of accommodation
structure.text: ## Article 19 - Criteria for the conferral of accommodation

nodes.title: 19.1 Reserves accommodation places
nodes.text:
### 19.1 Reserves accommodation places

ALiSEO reserves at its Residences no. 8 places for students enrolled in specialisation courses or PhD's (referred to in article 2 of this Notice); these places are assigned in increasing order of ISEEE/ISEEUP, with priority given to students enrolled in years subsequent to the first.

nodes.title: 19.2 Confirmation of subsequent years and first year master degree
nodes.text:
### 19.2 Confirmation of subsequent years and first year master degree

- 1. Students who have been awarded a scholarship and are included in the accommodation ranking as years following the first or “seventh semester + first year of the Master's degree”, who have already been assigned accommodation in the rankings of the 2024/2025 competition and who have not renounced to it or who have not been revoked during the 2024/2025 AY, are confirmed in the accommodation place occupied in the 2024/2025 AY (confirmations);
- 2. Students who have been awarded a scholarship and are included in the ranking as a first-year master's degree student, who have already been assigned accommodation in the rankings of the 2024/2025 competition and who have not renounced to it or who have not been revoked during the 2024/2025 AY, are confirmed in the accommodation place occupied in the 2024/2025 AY (confirmations), as long as they meet one of the following requirements


a) have completed their first level degree by 31 August 2025; b) be in possession of 135 credits as of 10 August 2025 (without the use of the bonus

credits).

nodes.title: 19.3 New requests of subsequent years
19.4 New requests of first years
nodes.text:
### 19.4 New requests of first years

1. The places made available after the assignment pursuant to paragraphs 19.1, 19.2 and 19.3 will be assigned as follows:

 70% to international EU students applying for benefits as a first-year (with priority given to students applying for benefits as a first-year master's degree);  30% to international non-EU students applying for benefits as a first year (with priority given to students applying for benefits as a first year master's degree student).

- 2. The ranking lists for the students referred to in paragraph 1 are formulated in ascending order of ISEEU/ISEEUP. In the event of a tie, the youngest student in terms of age takes precedence.
- 3. Any vacancies made available after assignments in accordance with the previous rankings can be redistributed to students eligible for housing on the basis of the percentages indicated above.

nodes.title: 19.5 Accommodation details
nodes.text:
### 19.5 Accommodation details

- 1. Disabled students referred to in article 13 of the present Notice shall be assigned accommodation with priority over other students. In addition, they are granted the right to choose between the allocation of accommodation and the cash supplement to the study grant, equal to the share of the Housing Service as per Table no. 8, if considered more suitable to their needs.
- 2. In the assignment of the accommodation place, the student who is found to be "Eligible" has priority over the student who is "Eligible Suspended".
- 3. The student in the condition of "Eligible Suspended" may be assigned accommodation when he/she corrects his/her position, within the time limits provided in relation to the type of suspension and within the limits of the availability of accommodation at the time the student corrects his/her position.
- 4. A student with a minor child who is a minor on the deadline date of this Notice and is present in Italy at the time of submission of the application in the student's household shall not be awarded housing, but shall be paid the money supplement to the scholarship, equal to the portion of the Accommodation Service set forth in Table No. 8, if he/she makes application for rental contribution as provided in paragraph 22.1 below.

structure.title: Article 20 - Method for the assignment of accommodation
structure.text: ## Article 20 - Method for the assignment of accommodation

nodes.title: 20.1 Manifestation of Interest
nodes.text:
### 20.1 Manifestation of Interest

- 1. Students eligible for accommodation at ALiSEO residences will receive at the e-mail address indicated on the application a request for expression of interest for accommodation.
- 2. To the students who have answered to the request for expressions of interest, a communication will be sent by email, with the indication of the assigned residence and the date of the convening, from the following dates:


- a) Starting from 15 September 2025 for CONFIRMATIONS subsequent years (UniGe and AFAM), “last semester” and “seventh semester + first year of master's degree” and first year of master's degree;
- b) Starting from 29 September 2025 NEW ENTRIES subsequent years (UniGe and AFAM), “last semester” and “seventh semester + first year of master's degree”; c) Starting from 6 October 2025 NEW ENTRIES first year undergraduate and second year graduate degrees (UniGe and AFAM); d) Starting from 6 October 2025 CONFIRMATIONS and NEW ENTRIES PhD students and specialists.


3. Students not attending, without justified reasons, by the summoning date indicated in the convening, will be considered as having forfeited the benefit.

Anyone who, for serious, documented reasons of health (certificate issued by the National Health Service), is unable to attend on the date indicated for entry, must submit a specific written request for postponement, duly documented, also emailing alloggi@aliseo.liguria.it. The competent service will evaluate the admissibility of requests received at its own discretion.

- 4. Accommodations in the ALISEO residences are assigned to students admitted, considering the nearness of the Department attended and the accommodation available, according to the priorities assigned to the individual rankings. The criterion of nearness to the Department will be subject, in the opinion of the Administration, to the need for cultural-ethnic integration of the communities present in the individual facilities.
- 5. The student, referred to in Paragraph 7 of Article 14 of this Notice, on "International Mobility" or "National Mobility" must vacate the accommodation place from his or her personal belongings before departure on Erasmus. A student who returns to the Residences after the spent period of "International Mobility" or "National Mobility" is reassigned an accommodation place only at the end of the mobility period. In reassignment, the accommodation place assigned before departure is not guaranteed.

nodes.title: 20.2 Accommodation check-in
nodes.text:
### 20.2 Accommodation check-in

1. At the moment of the check-in in the ALiSEO residences, students must:

- a) be in possession of a suitable valid identification document and residence permit for international non-EU students (the same as the one submitted in the online application when applying for benefits);
- b) having paid, via the PagoPA platform by accessing directly from the personal “Student Webdesk” page, the non-interest-bearing deposit of € 250,00 which will be refunded, at the request of the interested party, at the end of the right of use with reference to the entire university career.
- c) be in possession of the health documentation required by current regulations; d) sign a deed of acceptance of accommodation committing to comply with the Provisions on


Student Residences, downloadable from the institutional website of AliSEO.

-

nodes.title: 20.3 Leaving the accommodation before the summer break
nodes.text:
### 20.3 Leaving the accommodation before the summer break

- 1. By the closing date foreseen for the summer period (31 July 2026) all ALiSEO Student Residences must be left free of people and personal belongings.
- 2. Exclusively for students accommodated at the Milano-Terminus and Marinelle Residences the closing date for the summer period is anticipated to 24 July 2026.


-

nodes.title: 20.4 Revocation of benefits
nodes.text:
### 20.4 Revocation of benefits


1. Students subject to a measure of revocation of benefits (scholarship and accommodation) are required, for the period in which they were housed, to pay the value of the housing service according to the monthly rates indicated in Table n. 10 below, deducting any amount already paid:

Table n. 10

Single room in flat € 300,00 Single room in student house € 270,00 Double room in student house € 220,00 Double room in flat € 250,00

structure.title: Article 21 - Methods of payment for accommodation
structure.text:
## Article 21 - Methods of payment for accommodation

- 1. Payments for accommodation in ALiSEO residences must be made via the PagoPA platform by accessing directly from the personal “Sportello studenti”( “Student Webdesk”) page between the 5th and the 15th day of each month.
- 2. Delays in payment:


a) exceeding one month shall be sanctioned by the application of the late payment penalty provided for in the Provisions on Student Residences;

b) exceeding two months shall result in disqualification from the accommodation place and the initiation of legal proceedings to recover the debt.

structure.title: Article 22 - Rental contribution

nodes.title: 22.1 How to Apply
nodes.text:
### 22.1 How to Apply

- 1. In order to request this increase, known as the rentAL contribution, students must, by December 31, 2025:

- a) Apply exclusively through online procedure by logging on to www.aliseo.liguria.it. Student webdesk;

- b) upload a copy of the rental contract from which it is clear:  that the duration also partially includes the period 1 October 2025 – 31 July 2026;  that the students requesting the benefit are the holder of the contract. If they are not the holders of the contract, they must prove it by a deed of subentry into the contract in the name of another person, duly signed by the parties and registered, or appear in the contract as the person entitled to occupy the house and to pay a portion of the agreed fee.


- 2. In the event that the student has a rooming house or Residence/College contract, which is not subject to registration, he/she must upload the aforementioned document in which the clauses regulating the hospitality and the signing of the same are evidenced, by the date referred to in Paragraph 1 above.

nodes.title: 22.2 Payment
nodes.text:
### 22.2 Payment

- 1. Payment of the rental contribution is made within the terms and following the modalities of the second instalment of the scholarship for students enrolled in years subsequent to the first and within the terms and following the modalities of the scholarship disbursement for students enrolled in the first years and for students enrolled in PhD courses and specialization schools.
- 2. The amount of the rental contribution is determined by the number of months indicated on the rental contract or on the rooming house or Residence/College/Hostel contract in relation to the period of unavailability of the accommodation calculated over 10 months. Months are considered valid for the purposes of payment provided that the actual rental days are equal to or greater than fifteen.
- 3. The amount related to the rental contribution, determined based on paragraph 2 above, is paid upon uploading to the Student webdesk page at least one receipt showing payment of a monthly fee


- 4. Disabled students as referred to in art. 13 of this Notice who are eligible for accommodation and who opt for the cash supplement to the study scholarship (value attributed to the housing service as per Table no. 8) are not required to sign the contract referred to in paragraph 22.1, subsection 1 letter b.
- 5. A student with a minor child who is a minor on the deadline date of this Notice and is present in Italy at the time of submission of the application in the student's household may apply for the rental contribution by submitting an application as provided in paragraph 22.1 above.
- 6. Payment of monthly fees must be made by bank transfer, and for the purpose of disbursement of the contribution under this article, different kinds of payment cannot be accepted. The bank transfer may also be made by a third party, as long as it mentions the name of the student (the lessee indicated in the contract) requesting the rental contribution in the reason for payment. For the student who has a rooming house or Residence/College/Hostel contract, it is required to upload the receipts proving regular payment of the monthly rent. The student is required to upload all receipts evidencing monthly payments (for students who have already received scholarship payments, those following the disbursement) to the Student webdesk Page for the purposes of the controls referred to in paragraph 7 of this Notice below by August 31, 2026.
- 7. The competent Service carries out sample checks through verification of the receipts of payment, made by bank transfer in favour of the landlord, relating to the months of rent in relation to the months of rental contribution paid or to be paid. If, following the above-mentioned check, the student does not upload the payment receipts by August 31, 2026 or if a discrepancy is found regarding the number of months disbursed or to be disbursed as rental contribution, the competent Service shall revoke the benefit of the rental contribution or recalculate the amount on the basis of the receipts actually held by the student. If the rental contribution has already been paid, students are required to return the amount for which they are not entitled.

structure.title: Article 23 - Results of the competition and publication of rankings
structure.text:
## Article 23 - Results of the competition and publication of rankings

- 1.The notification of the approval of the competition rankings for the awarding of scholarships and the assignment of accommodation and supplement for international mobility programme as referred to art. 14 of the present Notice will be disclosed by means of publication on the website www.aliseo.liguria.it.

- 2. No individual communication will be sent. Students can consult their results in their webpage “Student webdesk”.
- 3. The results of competitions will be published, save for cases of force majeure, within the terms indicated in the following tables:

4. Any requests made for review against the results of the provisional rankings of the competition, prepared on plain paper, must be submitted to the Director of the ALiSEO Right to Study Area within 10 days of the date of publication. No requests for review will be considered regarding:

a) exclusion due to having submitted an application after the deadline; b) failure to be assigned on legitimate grounds.

The outcome of requests for review will be notified through the publication of the definitive rankings.

- 5. Students may appeal to T.A.R. Liguria (Regional Administrative Court) against definitive rankings within 60 days from the date of publication or they can present an extraordinary appeal to the Head of State within 120 days from the date of publication.
- 6. In provisional rankings for scholarships there are three possible outcomes: a) Eligible: student who meets the requirements in the present Notice and must wait for definitive rankings.


b) Eligible suspended: student who for particular situations can rectify their position under penalty of exclusion from the competition DEFINITIVELY within 24 October 2025, except for a student who:

- ◦ has applied for a first year Master's degree and is in the status of Suspended due to nonenrolment (must regularise his/her position by 10/3/2026);
- ◦ is undergoing an official check;
- ◦ is suspended for ‘undeclared foreign income’ as he must apply for ISEEUP certification (he must rectify his position by 31/12/2025);
- ◦ is suspended for “not registered” (must regularise his/her position by 31/12/2025); c) Not eligible: student who does not meet the requirements in the present Notice.


7. The following outcomes are possible in the provisional accommodation rankings: a) Eligible: student who meets the requirements provided for by this Notice and who therefore may become accommodated, if accommodation is available, upon acceptance;

b) Eligible suspended: a student who, due to particular situations, must rectify their position under penalty of being DEFINITIVELY excluded from the competition by 24 October 2025, except for a student who:

- ◦ has applied for a first year Master's degree and is in the status of Suspended due to nonenrolment (must regularise his/her position by 10/3/2026);
- ◦ is undergoing an official check;
- ◦ is suspended for ‘undeclared foreign income’ as he must apply for ISEEUP certification


(he must rectify his position by 31/12/2025); The student in ‘Suspended status’ will be contacted for the allocation of accommodation when he/she has regularised his/her position, and within the limits of the availability of accommodation, with the exception of students enrolled in the first year of a Master's degree course who are in Suspended status due to ‘non-enrolment’.

c) Not eligible: student who does not meet the requirements in the present Notice.

8. In definitive rankings for scholarships four outcomes are possible:

- a) Awarded scholarship: student who meets the requirements in the present Notice and has the right to the scholarship, without prejudice of further controls by the Economic Benefits Service.
- b) Awarded Suspended: student who is in the following situations:


• subject to ex officio verification;

- • suspended for "undeclared foreign income" as he/she has to apply for ISEEUP certificate by 31/12/2025;
- • suspended for "not enrolled" as he/she has to enroll by 31/12/2025 or by 10/03/2026 if he/ she has applied as a first-year master degree student; The above conditions of suspension must be remedied under penalty of revocation of benefits. c) Eligible: student who meets the requirements in the present Notice, but cannot benefit from scholarship, since all available scholarships have been awarded.


d) Not eligible: a student who does not meet the requirements set out in this notice or has not regularised his or her position as ‘Suspended’ in the provisional ranking by the deadline of 31/12/2025 (with the exception of the student who has applied as a first-year Master's degree student and is in the status of Suspended due to ‘non-enrolment’).

9.The following outcomes are possible in the final accommodation rankings: a) Eligible: student who meets the requirements provided for by this Notice and who therefore

may become accommodated, if accommodation is available, upon acceptance; b) Eligible suspended: student who is in the following situations:

- • subject to ex officio verification;
- • suspended for "undeclared foreign income" as he/she has to apply for ISEEUP certificate by 31/12/2025;
- • suspended for "not enrolled" as he/she has to enroll by 31/12/2025 or by 10/03/2026 if he/ she has applied as a first-year master degree student;


c) Not eligible: a student who does not meet the requirements set out in this notice or has not regularised his or her position as ‘Suspended’ in the provisional ranking by the deadline of 31/12/2025 (with the exception of the student who has applied as a first-year Master's degree student and is in the status of Suspended due to ‘non-enrolment’).

structure.title: Article 24 - Methods of payment of the scholarship
structure.text:
## Article 24 - Methods of payment of the scholarship

- 1. Payment of the scholarship is made only by transfers to a bank account or pre – paid card (whose ceiling allows the scholarship amount to be credited with Italian IBAN or belonging to the SEPA area. This account must be entitled to the student or has to be a joint account of the student requesting the benefit. If students indicate a foreign bank account, they must also indicate, in addition to the IBAN code, the BIC/SWIFT details of their account. ALiSEO is not responsible for any transfer costs charged by the Banks to the student.
- 2. The deadline for disbursement of the instalment is understood to be the date of the deed with which ALiSEO orders the payment. The actual availability of the sum on the student's current account will be during the following month, with variable timing depending on the technical times of the banks.
- 3. In the event of the death of the student, it should be noted that the use of the scholarship is a right linked to the status of the person and therefore not transferable by inheritance.
- 4. The first instalment of the scholarship, equal to 50% of the total, is calculated, for students applying for accommodation, on the basis of the scholarship for “off-site” students. If the students status changes to “commuting”, the relative positive balance, determined by the new status, is applied to the final instalment of the scholarship. If the above-mentioned balance is negative, the difference will be requested to the student.

nodes.title: 24.1 Students enrolled at subsequent years
nodes.text:

structure.title: Article 26 - Truthfulness checks on the produced declarations
structure.text:
## Article 26 - Truthfulness checks on the produced declarations

- 1. ALiSEO, in order to ensure compliance with the provisions of this Notice of Competition, shall carry out verifications of truthfulness of the self-certifications produced by the students in accordance with articles 43 and 71 of d.P.R. of 28 December 2000 no. 445 within the terms of the law and without the obligation to give notice to the student. Veracity checks, in accordance with the provisions of the Prime Ministerial Decree of 9 April 2001, are carried out using the method of verification with spot checks according to the provisions of the regulations in force. ALiSEO carries out the aforementioned checks starting from the date of closure of the beneficial competition and in compliance with the terms provided for by the relevant regulations in force.
- 2. In order to carry out the checks referred to in paragraph 1, in addition to requesting the student, via the e-mail address indicated by the same in the on-line application, all the documentation that


can prove the truthfulness of what has been self-certified, ALiSEO may carry out, by any means at its disposal the investigations it deems appropriate, also with the collaboration of the Universities, AFAM Institutions, the Bodies operating in the field of the right to study, of the competent State Authorities and Local Authorities, through access to the tax registry of the Revenue Agency and through the database of the INPS. In the cases envisaged by Article 21 octies of Law No. 241 of 7 August 1990, as amended and supplemented, ALiSEO shall issue the measure of revocation without the obligation to communicate the start of the assessment to the student. If the student has not communicated to ALiSEO the change of e-mail address or if the latter is incorrect, the communication sent to that address shall be considered valid for all purposes.

3. In the event of proven untruthful declarations, resulting in the enjoyment of benefits that are not due, pursuant to Article 10 of Legislative Decree no. 68 of 29 March 2012, the student forfeits the benefit and eligibility. The student may also be subject to an administrative penalty consisting in the payment of a sum of three times the amount received, both in monetary terms and in terms of the value of the services unduly used used. He/she also loses the right to obtain other payments for the entire duration of the course of study, without prejudice, in any case, to the application of sanctions. referred to in Article 38, paragraph 3 of Decree-Law No. 78 of 31 May 2010, converted with amendments by Law no. 122 of 30 July 2010, as well as the criminal provisions for facts constituting criminal offences. 4.The lists of students awarded the scholarship may be sent to the Financial Administration, the Italian Guardia di Finanza, the Italian National Welfare Institute (INPS), the Ministry of Universities and Research, the University and AFAM Institutions, the Italian Institutions for the Right to University Study, and the National Association for the Right to University Study Institutions (ANDISU). Members of the household of students benefiting from interventions that require a verification of financial conditions are included in the categories that are subject, pursuant to the current regulations, to the maximum controls.

-

structure.title: Article 27 - Reimbursement of the benefits
structure.text:
## Article 27 - Reimbursement of the benefits

- 1. Revocation of benefits is communicated to the email address indicated in the online application by the student. The revocation notice specifies the information relating to the amount to be reimbursed, the date by which make the payment and the relevant modalities of repayment. An appeal against the revocation decision may be submitted to the T.A.R. Liguria (Administrative Court of the Region of Liguria) within 60 days from the notification of the revocation or submit an extraordinary appeal to the President of the Italian Republic within 120 days from its publication. Instalments may be granted subject to authorisation by the Manager of the Right to Study Sector of ALiSEO, in accordance with the "Provisions on the recovery of scholarship credits, supplements and services enjoyed"in force. Payments relating to debts deriving from the revocation of benefits shall be made through the PagoPA platform by accessing directly from the personal "Student Webdesk" page.
- 2. In all cases of default in the repayment of sums due, the benefits granted are suspended until the debt position is regularised and, unless repayment plans have been agreed and the same are duly complied with, students in debt may not apply for benefits for future academic years.
- 3. A student who withdraws from the entire university career must refund the benefits obtained in the last year of enrolment (monetary amount of the scholarship, service fee).


-

structure.title: Article 28 - Reporting of a debt situation
structure.text:
## Article 28 - Reporting of a debt situation


1. In the event that the student is not in compliance with the payments due for any reason, ALiSEO, proceeds with the compulsory retrieval of the amount due through the Italian Revenue Agency Servizio Riscossioni (formerly Equitalia) and notifies the debt situation to the University and to the AFAM Institutions which, on the basis of specific agreements or understandings, may put in place measures that will not allow the student to proceed in his/her career until the debt is regularised.

-

structure.title: Article 29 - Reporting obligations and modalities
structure.text:
## Article 29 - Reporting obligations and modalities

- 1. Students are required to inform ALiSEO of any change relevant to the benefits granted under the provisions of the present Notice, which occurs after the date of submission of the application (e.g. award of a scholarship or other financial aid, leave for another university/institution AFAM, renunciation of studies, non-enrolment, change of residence, etc.).
- 2. All communications from ALiSEO, including those related to assessments and withdrawal of benefits take place via the email address entered in the ALiSEO database by the student. Such communications have to all legal effects, replacing the postal communication.
- 3. If the student does not communicate the change of e-mail address or the same is incorrect, the communication from by ALiSEO (including those related to the assessment and revocation of benefits) will be considered valid for all purposes if it was notified to the last e-mail address communicated to the Agency by the student.
- 4. The student must be aware that: a) he/she is identified, as regards correspondence by e-mail, through the e-mail address present in the ALiSEO database; b) the e-mail address present in the ALiSEO database is attributed to the student and must be managed by the student himself/herself and not to any other party; c) all communications between the student and ALiSEO and vice versa must be made to the address entered in the ALiSEO database; d) failure to read communications sent to the student by ALiSEO does not constitute grounds acceptable for any dispute; e) e-mails sent by the student from different addresses other than the one entered in the ALiSEO database will not be taken into consideration and therefore will not be answered by ALiSEO; f) any change of the e-mail address must be made by the student using the appropriate procedure available on the student's personal page "Student Webdesk".


-

structure.title: Article 30 - Provisions on privacy protection
structure.text:
## Article 30 - Provisions on privacy protection


- 1. The data will be processed in accordance with EU Regulation 2016/679 and Legislative Decree no. 196/03 and ss.mm.ii as amended by Legislative Decree no. 101 of 10 August 2018. In the online application students must sign the acknowledgement of the information notice; the provision of personal data as well as the declaration of having read the information notice is mandatory for the participation in the competition.
- 2. The data requested in self-certifications (e.g. personal data, residence, e-mail address, registration data, etc.) as well as those contained in the requested documentation (income data, etc.) are intended for all operations, normally carried out by electronic and automated means, aimed at the processing of rankings for the allocation of scholarships, university accommodation and for the provision of the catering service referred to in the Prime Ministerial Decree of 9 April 2001, Legislative Decree 29 March 2012, no. 68 and Ministry of University and Research Decree no. 1320 of 17 December 2021 and for surveys quality and cognitive surveys, also anonymous, aimed at improving the services of ALiSEO The legal basis of data processing is the performance of a task of public interest provided for by the aforementioned legislation.
- 3. The data may be communicated to the Region of Liguria, to the Financial Administration, to the Italian Finance Police, to the talian National Welfare Institute (INPS), to the Ministry of University and Research, to the University and AFAM Institutions, to the Italian National Association for the Right to University Studies (ANDISU), to the Ministry of the Interior, to the Ministry of Defence, as well as to other public and/or private entities for control purposes.


- 4. The data will be stored for ten years at the end of processing, unless otherwise stipulated by the Ministry of University and Research and the Region.
- 5. Data subjects are guaranteed the rights set out in Articles 15 et seq. of EU Regulation 2016/679; the data subject has also the right to lodge a complaint with the Data Protection Authority.
- 6. The data controller is ALiSEO, Agenzia Ligure per gli studenti e l'orientamento, based in via San Vincenzo, Genova. ALiSEO has appointed as data protection officer Avv. Sofia Piermattei, who can be contacted at privacy@wslegal.it.

- 7. The extended information notice will be available in the online application form pursuant to this Notice.
